articles.title: New dime to celebrate America’s birthday takes on new meaning under Trump - The Washington Post
articles.description: The redesign of the dime was first recommended in 2024, but critics suggest it is the newest symbol of a more aggressive kind of nationalism championed by Trump.

articles.title: Fears for press freedom as billionaire takes control of East Africa's largest media house - BBC
articles.description: Rostam Aziz's purchase raises questions about continued journalistic independence in the region.

articles.title: Gov. Hobbs signs new Arizona law requiring penny-rounding at checkout - AZ Family
articles.description: Gov. Katie Hobbs signed a new law Friday that forces businesses to round if pennies aren't available.

articles.title: Economy was shakier than it appeared heading into Iran conflict - The Washington Post
articles.description: The U.S. economy was in worse shape in the weeks before the U.S. and Israel launched strikes against Iran than earlier government estimates had suggested.

articles.title: Yen Slides to Weakest Since July 2024 as Iran War Escalates - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: The dollar ended a volatile week at its highest level this year, underscoring renewed appetite for the greenback as a haven amid the escalating conflict with Iran and surging energy prices.

articles.title: Internet raises $500k for elderly couple doing DoorDash to make ends meet - Dexerto
articles.description: Richard Pulley, a 78-year-old, has gone viral online after doing food deliveries to make ends meet, and the internet is rallying to raise money for him so he can actually retire for good.

articles.title: Amazon Raises Price Of Ad-Free Prime Video Plan, Tags “Ultra” Onto Brand Name - Deadline
Costco shopper is suing the retailer to get piece of $180 billion in tariff refunds - Fortune
articles.description: A Costco shopper sued the big-box retailer, saying a proposed class of plaintiffs could top 100, and they’re demanding $5 million in tariff refunds.

articles.title: Honda Denies Killing The Prologue EV - Motor1.com
articles.description: A new report suggests Honda will end Prologue production in December, with no replacement model planned, as it shifts away from electric vehicles.
